headline: Chip stocks start Q3 with a dud after record Q2 rallies
detail: After a huge second quarter, semiconductor names are pulling back to open Q3. This directly pressures SMH, SOXL, and your broader tech concentration. On margin, even a modest sector-wide exhale creates outsized account pain.
rec: No new action yet - watch whether SMH holds above its recent base. If SMH accelerates lower toward $580s, tighten your SOXL stop closer to $210 rather than waiting for $200.

headline: Meta pops 9% on cloud compute push
detail: Meta surging on a cloud pivot is a positive signal for AI infrastructure demand, which feeds into chip and semiconductor names broadly. It's not a direct holding but it lifts sentiment for the AI trade that underpins CRWD and your semis. One strong name doesn't reverse a sector dud day though.
rec: CRWD is your cleanest winner at +7.4% - no reason to touch it today, but if this AI/cloud optimism builds, it's the position you let run. No new buy warranted on this headline alone.

headline: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI and Anthropic token model
detail: Karp's criticism highlights fractures in how AI companies monetize their technology. This is more noise than signal for your specific holdings today, but ongoing AI business model uncertainty can create volatility across the sector you're concentrated in.
rec: None of your holdings are directly Palantir or OpenAI-exposed. This is background context - watch how AI sentiment evolves but no trade warranted from this headline today.

headline: Dan Ives exits Wedbush for new venture
detail: Ives is one of the most followed tech bulls on Wall Street - his departure from Wedbush could mean slightly less institutional cheerleading for big tech names in the near term. Minor sentiment item, not a fundamental shift.
rec: No trade here. Worth knowing if you follow his coverage, but this doesn't change any level in your book today.
